question.text: What is the expiry day for GOI bond futures (6-year, 10-year, and 13-year) contracts?

answer.text: Last Thursday of the contract month
Last Wednesday of the contract month
Last Friday of the contract month
Last business day of the contract month

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** GOI bond futures (across all tenors — 6-year, 10-year, and 13-year) expire on the last Thursday of the contract month. This is consistent across all GOI bond futures products.
**The Trap:** Last Wednesday is for 91-day T-Bill futures. Last Friday and last business day are not correct for any GOI bond futures. Note that MIBOR futures expire on the last working day. Each product has a specific expiry day.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Expiry day details are frequently tested. Compare: Equity options — last Thursday (monthly), GOI bond futures — last Thursday, T-Bill futures — last Wednesday, MIBOR futures — last working day.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If the last Thursday is a trading holiday, the contract expires on the previous trading day. Always check the exchange's holiday calendar near expiry dates.
